    1. executeBuild(Consumer, String[])

Class BuildController<T>

java.lang.Object
org.copalis.jam.cli.BuildController<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the project interface type
public class BuildController<T> extends Object
A build process command-line argument parser and controller. The controller is constructed with a reference to a project interface which defines the build targets and logic.

When the controller is constructed it uses Memorizer to create a memoized instance of the project interface and registers itself as an observer so that it can intercept and log all method calls.

Targets

A method is exposed as a build target if it has 0 parameters. For example, the method 
 default File jarFile() { ... }
defines a build target called jarFile.

Build targets, and they methods they call, should generally be idempotent and return a reference to files they retrieve or create. Return values which implement the Mutable interface allow for file modification to be detected and enable incremental builds.

Build targets with void return type are a special case; they are not cached, and may be used for side-effects. For example 

 default void clean() { ... }

Incremental builds

At the end of a build the state of the memoizer's cache is saved to a file called .<project>.ser, where <project> is the unqualified name of the project interface. Subsequent builds load the previous build's cache and check for modifications to mutable dependencies so that target executions only rebuild artifacts which are stale.
See Also:
  • Command-line options
  • Details of caching behavior

  • Constructor Details

    • BuildController

      public BuildController(Class<T> type)

  • Method Details

    • executeBuild

      public void executeBuild(Consumer<T> buildFn, String[] args)
      Executes a build as specified by command-line arguments.

      Depending on the command-line arguments supplied to this method, either target methods on the project interface are executed or information about the build or its status is displayed to the console.

      Command-line arguments may be

      --help
      Displays help information
      --cache
      Displays the contents of the memoizer cache
      --targets
      Displays the names, return types, and cache status of the target methods
      target-name
      Executes the target method with the specified name
      If no arguments are specified, buildFn is invoked.
      Parameters:
      buildFn - a consumer that invokes the default build target
      args - the build's command line arguments.
